sections.past: EUR/USD slipped 19 pips to 1.1716 as the dollar steadied ahead of the Trump-Xi Beijing summit, trimming Monday's risk-on rally.
sections.why: The planned trade truce extension is keeping a floor under risk sentiment, but euro upside is capped by soft EZ industrial data and Warsh's hawkish confirmation as new Fed chair.
sections.watch: Trump-Xi outcomes dominate — any concrete tariff rollback language sends EUR toward 1.1780; a breakdown reopens 1.1650.

sections.past: Cable dipped 15 pips to 1.3522 in thin trade, with UK political noise around Starmer's leadership crisis providing a mild headwind.
sections.why: Sterling remains resilient on a macro basis — BoE rate differentials favor the pound — but domestic political uncertainty is capping gains above 1.3560.
sections.watch: UK labor market data due Thursday; a hot wages print would reinforce BoE hawkishness and push cable toward 1.3600.

sections.past: USD/JPY edged up 19 pips to 157.86 as US 10Y yields climbed to 4.48%, widening the rate differential against the BoJ's ultra-loose stance.
sections.why: New Fed chair Warsh's hawkish rhetoric on inflation is pushing the long end higher, but yen weakness past 158 risks verbal intervention from Tokyo.
sections.watch: US Retail Sales at 15:30 TRT — a strong print extends the yield push toward 158.50; BoJ commentary is the wildcard.

sections.past: AUD/USD rallied 26 pips to 0.7264, outperforming G10 peers as copper and iron ore firmed on China stimulus hopes ahead of the Trump-Xi summit.
sections.why: Australia is the direct proxy trade for US-China detente — any tariff relief lifts commodity demand expectations and pulls AUD higher.
sections.watch: Australian employment data Thursday plus Trump-Xi meeting outcomes — a double catalyst that could drive a 50-pip range.

sections.past: Gold held flat at $4,699, consolidating near all-time highs after last week's surge through $4,700 faded into profit-taking.
sections.why: The push-pull between geopolitical safe-haven flows and rising real yields is keeping gold pinned — DXY at 98.48 provides a tailwind, but 10Y at 4.48% caps upside.
sections.watch: Trump-Xi summit is the key risk event — genuine de-escalation would trigger gold selling toward $4,630; any breakdown sends it back above $4,750.

sections.past: Silver dropped 1.3% to $87.71, underperforming gold as the gold-silver ratio widened on profit-taking in industrial metals.
sections.why: Silver's dual identity hurts here — industrial demand uncertainty from US-China tensions offsets monetary haven support, while the AI-driven solar panel build-out trade pauses for breath.
sections.watch: China industrial production data Friday and the Trump-Xi tariff discussions will determine if silver's industrial bid resumes or fades.

sections.past: WTI crude ticked up 0.2% to $101.22 as the Iran-Israel ceasefire held but traders stayed cautious ahead of the Beijing summit.
sections.why: Oil is caught between Middle East supply risk premium and demand uncertainty — Brent above $106 reflects the geopolitical floor, but a tariff deal could boost demand expectations.
sections.watch: EIA crude inventories at 17:30 TRT plus Trump-Xi trade headlines — a drawdown plus positive trade signals could push WTI toward $103.

sections.past: S&P 500 gained 0.6% to 7,444, posting fresh all-time highs as tech led a broad risk-on session ahead of the Trump-Xi summit.
sections.why: The AI capex cycle is broadening — Nebius surging 684% on data center revenue shows the infrastructure buildout is accelerating beyond the Mag 7.
sections.watch: US Retail Sales Thursday at 15:30 TRT and ongoing earnings from Cisco (beat on AI orders) set the tone — 7,500 is the next psychological level.

sections.past: Nasdaq 100 surged 1.0% to 29,367, led by GOOGL (+3.9%) and TSLA (+2.7%) as the AI trade broadened with Cerebras IPO hype.
sections.why: The comparison to the dot-com bubble is losing traction — AI revenue is real (Nebius, Cisco AI orders), and the Cerebras blockbuster IPO signals a deepening capital market appetite.
sections.watch: Trump-Xi meeting could catalyze chip sector if AI safeguard agreements ease export restrictions — watch SOX index for confirmation above 7,000.

sections.past: Dow slipped 0.1% to 49,693, lagging as value stocks paused while growth and tech outperformed on AI momentum.
sections.why: Rotation into tech/growth names is pulling capital from Dow industrials — Cisco jumped on AI orders but broader industrial sentiment is cautious ahead of trade talks.
sections.watch: 50,000 remains the key psychological barrier — a positive Trump-Xi trade deal outcome is the catalyst most likely to break it.

sections.past: Bitcoin rose 0.7% to $79,797 despite Bitcoin ETFs recording their largest outflow in three months, with spot buying absorbing the institutional selling.
sections.why: BTC dominance at 58.2% shows capital concentration in bitcoin over altcoins — the ETF outflow signals institutional profit-taking, but on-chain accumulation addresses keep climbing.
sections.watch: The $80,000 level is the immediate resistance — a clean break above with ETF inflow reversal targets $83,000; rejection sends it back to $77,000 support.

sections.past: ETH gained 0.5% to $2,268, modestly outperforming amid renewed attention on Ethereum's AI-compute narrative.
sections.why: Bit Digital's pivot toward AI infrastructure is highlighting the ETH-AI convergence trade, but low gas fees and declining DeFi TVL keep structural bulls waiting for the Pectra upgrade catalysts.
sections.watch: ETH/BTC ratio is the key signal — a bounce off 0.0284 suggests altcoin rotation is starting; failure opens 0.0270.

sections.past: NVIDIA surged 2.3% to $225.83 as Jensen Huang joined Trump's delegation to Beijing, signaling potential AI export policy shifts.
sections.why: Huang's presence at the Trump-Xi meeting is the most bullish signal for chip export relief in months — if AI safeguard agreements ease restrictions, NVDA's China TAM re-opens significantly.
sections.watch: Trump-Xi AI safeguard discussions Thursday are the make-or-break — positive language on chip exports targets $235; disappointment sends it back to $218.

sections.past: Tesla jumped 2.7% to $445.27, rallying with the broader EV sector as Musk confirmed attendance at the Trump-Xi Beijing meeting.
sections.why: Musk's diplomatic role gives Tesla unique access to Chinese policy discussions — any tariff relief on EV components or Shanghai factory expansion signals would be directly accretive.
sections.watch: Tesla's China sales data and Trump-Xi EV-related trade concessions are the dual catalysts — $460 resistance from April highs is the target if talks go well.

sections.past: Apple rose 1.4% to $298.87, hitting a fresh all-time high as Tim Cook joined the Trump delegation to Beijing.
sections.why: Apple's supply chain is the most China-exposed of the Mag 7 — Cook's presence at trade talks signals Apple is actively lobbying for tariff relief on iPhone components.
sections.watch: The $300 round number is immediate resistance — a break above on positive trade headlines would be technically significant, opening $310.

sections.past: Microsoft dipped 0.6% to $405.21, giving back gains as Anthropic's Claude for Small Business launch intensified the AI platform competition narrative.
sections.why: MSFT's AI moat through OpenAI partnership is being tested from multiple angles — Anthropic, Google, and now open-source models are narrowing the enterprise AI premium.
sections.watch: Azure growth guidance at the next earnings call is what matters — until then, $400 is support and $415 is the ceiling.

sections.past: Meta jumped 2.3% to $616.63, outperforming on a Wall Street note calling it a bargain relative to its AI advertising moat.
sections.why: At ~22x forward earnings with AI-driven ad targeting improvements flowing straight to margins, Meta is being re-rated as a value play within the Mag 7.
sections.watch: Reels monetization data and any updates on the Llama 4 open-source rollout at upcoming events — $630 is the next resistance from February highs.

sections.past: Alphabet surged 3.9% to $402.62, leading the Mag 7 as Apple's fresh record and broad tech optimism lifted the search giant.
sections.why: Google's AI Overviews are driving search revenue re-acceleration, and the Waymo autonomous vehicle narrative adds a second growth vector that the market is beginning to price in.
sections.watch: Antitrust ruling timeline and Google I/O follow-up commentary on Gemini adoption are the near-term catalysts — $410 resistance, $390 support.

sections.past: Amazon gained 1.6% to $270.13, extending its streak to six consecutive quarters of earnings growth as AWS AI demand stays robust.
sections.why: AWS is the pick-and-shovel play for the AI buildout — six straight quarters of accelerating revenue growth proves the hyperscaler thesis, and Nebius's data center surge confirms the broader demand.
sections.watch: Retail Sales data Thursday at 15:30 TRT is a dual catalyst — strong consumer spending helps retail while validating the AWS infrastructure spending cycle.
